body:
Title: CRA 2025 T1 federal net tax aggregation.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/en/revenue-agency/services/forms-publications/tax-packages-years/general-income-tax-benefit-package/5000-r.html
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5000-r/5000-r-25e.txt

CRA Form 5000-R Income Tax and Benefit Return for 2025 Step 5 Part C calculates net federal tax. Line 119 enters the amount from line 76. Line 120, line 40424, is federal tax on split income from Form T1206. Line 121, line 40400, adds line 119 and line 120.

Line 122 is the amount from line 35000. Line 123, line 40425, is the federal dividend tax credit. Line 124, line 40427, is minimum tax carryover. Line 125 adds lines 122 to 124. Line 126, line 42900, subtracts line 125 from line 121 and floors negative amounts at zero as basic federal tax.

Line 127 is federal surtax on income earned outside Canada from Form T2203. Line 128 adds line 126 and line 127. Line 129, line 40500, is the federal foreign tax credit from Form T2209. Line 130 subtracts line 129 from line 128. Line 131 is recapture of investment tax credit from Form T2038(IND). Line 132 adds line 130 and line 131. Line 133 is federal logging tax credit. Line 134, line 40600, subtracts line 133 from line 132 and floors negative amounts at zero as federal tax.

Line 135, line 41000, is the federal political contribution tax credit. Line 136, line 41200, is the investment tax credit. Line 137, line 41400, is the labour-sponsored funds tax credit. Line 138, line 41600, adds lines 135 to 137. Line 139, line 41700, subtracts line 138 from line 134 and floors negative amounts at zero. Line 140, line 41500, is advanced Canada workers benefit. Line 141, line 41800, is special taxes. Line 142, line 42000, adds lines 139 to 141 as net federal tax.
